decisions.title: Commit to Sift Edge GA timeline for Q2 2026
decisions.context: ENG-8855 syncing prototype in progress. Edge is critical moat-builder but needs clear GA date to align customer pilots and sales commitments.

arguments.for: Edge prototype shows technical feasibility. Customer demand validated. Moat pillar depends on edge deployment capability.
arguments.against: Syncing complexity unknown. Performance at scale untested. May need Q3 buffer for hardening.

decisions.title: Prioritize performance work over new features in Explore V2
decisions.context: Multiple performance issues surfaced: ENG-8960 channel list CPU, ENG-8874 phase annotations latency, ENG-8957 calculated channels latency, ENG-8958 structured data latency. Core experience degrading.

arguments.for: Nail the Core requires fast, reliable experience. Performance debt compounds. Customers report slowness impacting decisions velocity.
arguments.against: Feature roadmap commitments to enterprise customers. Competitive pressure to ship Canvas integrations and Python Rules.

decisions.title: Standardize on Python Rules as primary rule authoring experience
decisions.context: ENG-8256 family view rule creation, ENG-8910 sift_py client migration suggests Python becoming core workflow. Need decision on visual vs code-first strategy.

arguments.for: Engineers prefer code. Version control integration. More expressive. Differentiator vs competitors. Embed Deeper into eng workflows.
arguments.against: Non-technical users need visual tools. Learning curve steeper. Support burden increases. May fragment user base.

decisions.title: Ship Family Config V1 this sprint vs add more polish
decisions.context: ENG-8952 family config polish and ENG-7852 preview stats in progress. Feature 90% done but team wants more refinement before release.

arguments.for: Signal>Evidence>Decision means ship and learn. 3 customers waiting. Polish can follow based on usage data.
arguments.against: First impression matters. Enterprise buyers expect polish. Rework costs more than getting it right first time.

decisions.title: Allocate dedicated team to API performance optimization
decisions.context: Four separate API latency tickets (ENG-8874, 8957, 8958, 8299 export memory). Systemic performance issues suggest architectural problem, not isolated bugs.
decisions.blockedBy: Need headcount approval and team restructure plan

arguments.for: Performance is existential for Decisions Per Customer metric. Current approach reactive and slow. Dedicated focus will 10x progress.
arguments.against: Team already stretched. May delay Canvas and Ask SIFT roadmaps. Performance optimization less visible to customers than features.

decisions.title: Deprecate legacy export flow after memory fix ships
decisions.context: ENG-8299 fixing export memory but maintaining two export paths increases complexity and slows future iteration.

arguments.for: Technical debt reduction. Faster iteration. Clearer user experience. Forces migration to better architecture.
arguments.against: Some customers rely on old export format. Breaking change requires customer comms and migration support. Risk churn.

timeAudit.weekSummary: Performance crisis demanding 60% reactive allocation. Only 20% time on strategic moat-building.
alerts: Thursday entirely booked with performance war rooms
No focus blocks scheduled Friday - recommend blocking AM
3 decision deadlines this week but no decision review meeting scheduled
Performance work displacing Canvas roadmap review (was scheduled for Tuesday)
